tasks.title: Add inventory validation notes
tasks.content:
Inventory validation should happen before running playbooks against production hosts. A small mistake in group membership or host variables can apply roles to the wrong server.

Validation checks:

- expected host groups exist
- hostnames and IPs are documented
- SSH user is explicit
- production and sample inventories are separate
- sensitive variables are not committed in plain text
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows operational caution around Ansible-driven server changes.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it separates sample and production inventory concerns.

tasks.title: Add backup restore runbook outline
tasks.content:
A backup is only useful when restore has been tested. Restore documentation should be available beside backup automation.

Restore runbook outline:

- identify target backup artifact
- provision or isolate restore target
- load required credentials securely
- run restore command
- verify application-level data integrity
- document recovery time and recovery point
tasks.portfolio_reason: Balances backup automation with real recovery readiness.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it includes verification after restore.

tasks.title: Add SSH hardening notes
tasks.content:
SSH hardening should be staged carefully so automation access is not accidentally locked out.

Recommended sequence:

- confirm non-root sudo user access
- install and test SSH keys
- restrict password authentication after key access works
- limit SSH networks where possible
- keep a break-glass access path documented

files.content:
SSH hardening should be staged carefully so automation access is not accidentally locked out.

Recommended sequence:

- confirm non-root sudo user access
- install and test SSH keys
- restrict password authentication after key access works
- limit SSH networks where possible
- keep a break-glass access path documented

PasswordAuthentication no
PermitRootLogin no
PubkeyAuthentication yes
X11Forwarding no
AllowTcpForwarding no
ClientAliveInterval 300
ClientAliveCountMax 2

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class SshHardeningTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_disables_password_and_root_login(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/common/templates/sshd-hardening.conf.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("PasswordAuthentication no", content)
        self.assertIn("PermitRootLogin no", content)

    def test_template_keeps_key_authentication_enabled(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/common/templates/sshd-hardening.conf.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("PubkeyAuthentication yes", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows production-safe Linux hardening thinking, not just a checklist.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it warns against locking out automation.

tasks.title: Document Docker host baseline
tasks.content:
Docker hosts should have a predictable baseline before application deployment.

Baseline items:

- Docker service enabled and monitored
- compose plugin available
- app deployment user has required permissions
- log rotation is configured
- disk usage is monitored
- container restart policy is documented
tasks.portfolio_reason: Connects Linux bootstrap automation with Docker Compose production workloads.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it avoids environment-specific secrets.

files.content:
Docker hosts should have a predictable baseline before application deployment.

Baseline items:

- Docker service enabled and monitored
- compose plugin available
- app deployment user has required permissions
- log rotation is configured
- disk usage is monitored
- container restart policy is documented

- name: Ensure Docker configuration directory exists
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/docker
    state: directory
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Install Docker daemon baseline configuration
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: daemon.json.j2
    dest: /etc/docker/daemon.json
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class DockerBaselineTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_daemon_template_enables_live_restore_and_log_rotation(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/docker/templates/daemon.json.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn('"live-restore": true', content)
        self.assertIn('"max-size": "100m"', content)
        self.assertIn('"max-file": "3"', content)

    def test_baseline_task_installs_daemon_config(self):
        task = ROOT / "roles/docker/tasks/baseline.yml"
        content = task.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("daemon.json.j2", content)
        self.assertIn("/etc/docker/daemon.json", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add Node Exporter alert planning notes
tasks.content:
Node Exporter metrics become more useful when paired with baseline alerts.

Starter alert areas:

- disk space and inode pressure
- high load average
- memory pressure
- network saturation
- filesystem read-only state
- systemd service failures where collected separately
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows that monitoring installation is tied to actionable operations.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm alert areas are host-level and actionable.

files.content:
Node Exporter metrics become more useful when paired with baseline alerts.

Starter alert areas:

- disk space and inode pressure
- high load average
- memory pressure
- network saturation
- filesystem read-only state
- systemd service failures where collected separately

groups:
  - name: node-exporter-baseline
    rules:
      - alert: HostDiskSpaceLow
        expr: node_filesystem_avail_bytes / node_filesystem_size_bytes < 0.15
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: Host disk space is below baseline threshold
      - alert: HostMemoryPressure
        expr: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes / node_memory_MemTotal_bytes < 0.10
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: Host memory pressure is above baseline threshold

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class NodeExporterAlertsTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_alert_template_contains_disk_and_memory_alerts(self):
        template = ROOT / "roles/node_exporter/templates/node-exporter-alerts.yml.j2"
        content = template.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("HostDiskSpaceLow", content)
        self.assertIn("HostMemoryPressure", content)
        self.assertIn("node_filesystem_avail_bytes", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add Ansible Vault guidance
tasks.content:
Secrets should be kept out of inventories and role defaults. Ansible Vault can protect sensitive values while keeping automation repeatable.

Vault usage guidance:

- store secrets in encrypted variable files
- keep sample variables non-sensitive
- document required variable names
- avoid putting vault passwords in the repository
- test playbooks with a non-production vault first
tasks.portfolio_reason: Improves the security story for Ansible-based operational automation.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it does not include real secret examples.

files.content:
Secrets should be kept out of inventories and role defaults. Ansible Vault can protect sensitive values while keeping automation repeatable.

Vault usage guidance:

- store secrets in encrypted variable files
- keep sample variables non-sensitive
- document required variable names
- avoid putting vault passwords in the repository
- test playbooks with a non-production vault first

# Encrypt the real file with ansible-vault before production use.
postgres_password: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT
deploy_private_key: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT
backup_encryption_key: CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]

        content = example.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("CHANGE_ME_WITH_ANSIBLE_VAULT", content)
        self.assertNotIn("password123", content.lower())

    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add sudoers drop-in policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows safer privilege boundaries for deployment users on Linux hosts.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm sudoers drop-in does not grant broad ALL privileges.

files.content:
Deployment users should receive narrow sudo permissions instead of broad root access. A sudoers drop-in can document the exact operational commands allowed for automation.

Policy checks:

- deploy user variable is explicit
- allowed commands are narrow
- no blanket `ALL` command grant
- file mode is managed by Ansible

# Managed by Ansible. Validate with visudo before enabling.
{{ deploy_user }} ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart docker, /usr/bin/systemctl reload nginx

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]

        content = (ROOT / "roles/common/templates/sudoers-deploy.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("{{ deploy_user }}", content)
        self.assertIn("/usr/bin/systemctl reload nginx", content)
        self.assertNotIn("NOPASSWD: ALL", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add firewall baseline policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows host hardening includes explicit inbound access rules, not only package installation.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm SSH and application ports are explicit.

files.content:
A firewall baseline should make inbound access explicit. SSH should be limited to trusted networks, and application ports should be declared by variable rather than hidden in role logic.

Baseline rules:

- default deny inbound
- allow trusted SSH networks
- allow declared application ports
- keep rule comments reviewable

# Managed by Ansible
default deny incoming
default allow outgoing
allow from {{ trusted_ssh_cidr }} to any port 22 proto tcp comment 'trusted ssh'
{% for port in app_tcp_ports | default([]) %}
allow {{ port }}/tcp comment 'application tcp port'
{% endfor %}

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]

        content = (ROOT / "roles/firewall/templates/ufw-baseline.rules.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("default deny incoming", content)
        self.assertIn("trusted_ssh_cidr", content)
        self.assertIn("app_tcp_ports", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.title: Add systemd service policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows Linux bootstrap roles manage service reliability instead of only installing packages.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm restart policy is explicit.

files.content:
Systemd units for platform services should include restart behavior, dependency ordering, and log visibility. This makes service recovery predictable after host changes.

Policy checks:

- restart policy
- restart delay
- wanted-by target
- environment file path

[Unit]
Description={{ service_description | default('Platform service') }}
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
EnvironmentFile={{ service_environment_file }}
ExecStart={{ service_exec_start }}
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

import unittest

tasks.title: Add sysctl hardening policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows host bootstrap includes kernel networking hardening.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm key sysctl settings are templated.

files.content:
Sysctl hardening should define baseline kernel networking protections for Linux hosts. Settings should be templated and reviewed before production rollout.

Baseline settings:

- disable IP forwarding by default
- disable source routing
- enable reverse path filtering
- ignore ICMP redirects

net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

import unittest

        self.assertIn("net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0", content)
        self.assertIn("rp_filter = 1", content)
        self.assertIn("accept_redirects = 0", content)

tasks.title: Add backup restore drill policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows server bootstrap includes recoverability checks instead of backup creation alone.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm the restore drill script validates target and manifest inputs.

files.content:
Backup restore drill policy should verify that backup artifacts can be restored into a safe target. Operators should test restore behavior before relying on backups in incidents.

Drill inputs:

- backup manifest path
- restore target
- dry-run flag
- operator

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MANIFEST="${1:?backup manifest path is required}"
TARGET="${2:?restore target is required}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-true}"

if [[ ! -f "$MANIFEST" ]]; then
  echo "backup manifest not found: $MANIFEST" >&2
  exit 2
fi

if [[ "$TARGET" == "/" ]]; then
  echo "refusing to restore into filesystem root" >&2
  exit 3
fi

echo "restore drill manifest=$MANIFEST target=$TARGET dry_run=$DRY_RUN"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class BackupRestoreDrillTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_restore_drill_template_has_guardrails(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/common/templates/backup-restore-drill.sh.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("backup manifest path is required", content)
        self.assertIn("refusing to restore into filesystem root", content)
        self.assertIn("DRY_RUN", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

files.content:
Systemd services should restart predictably without hiding crash loops. A small restart-safety drop-in can bound restart bursts and make failures visible to operators.

Recommended controls:

- restart on failure only
- short but bounded restart delay
- start limit interval
- start limit burst
- clear tests for generated unit policy

        self.assertIn("RestartSec", content)

tasks.title: Add backup healthcheck hook template

files.content:
Backup jobs should emit a lightweight health signal after a successful run. This gives monitoring a simple way to detect stale or failing backups without reading large log files.

Hook behavior:

- fail when backup artifact path is missing
- write a timestamped status file
- include backup artifact name
- keep output free of credentials

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${BACKUP_ARTIFACT:?BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required}"
STATUS_FILE="{{ backup_healthcheck_status_file | default('/var/lib/platform-backup/last-success.txt') }}"
install -d -m 0750 "$(dirname "$STATUS_FILE")"
printf 'backup_artifact=%s\ncompleted_at=%s\n' "$(basename "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT")" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" > "$STATUS_FILE"
chmod 0640 "$STATUS_FILE"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class BackupHealthcheckTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_requires_artifact_and_writes_status(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/backup/templates/backup-healthcheck.sh.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required", content)
        self.assertIn("last-success.txt", content)
        self.assertIn("completed_at", content)
        self.assertNotIn("PGPASSWORD", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Strengthens backup operational readiness with a monitorable success signal.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm the hook requires an artifact and avoids credentials.

tasks.title: Add backup artifact permission policy

files.content:
Backup artifacts should be created with restrictive ownership and permissions from the start. The bootstrap role can provide a small helper that verifies the backup directory, artifact mode, and group are explicit without placing credentials in scripts.

Policy controls:

- backup directory mode is 0750
- backup artifacts use mode 0640
- owner and group are configurable
- script fails when artifact path is missing

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${BACKUP_ARTIFACT:?BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required}"
BACKUP_OWNER="{{ backup_owner | default('root') }}"
BACKUP_GROUP="{{ backup_group | default('platform-backup') }}"
install -d -m 0750 -o "$BACKUP_OWNER" -g "$BACKUP_GROUP" "$(dirname "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT")
chown "$BACKUP_OWNER:$BACKUP_GROUP" "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT"
chmod 0640 "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class BackupArtifactPermissionTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_uses_restrictive_modes_without_credentials(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/backup/templates/backup-artifact-permissions.sh.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required", content)
        self.assertIn("-m 0750", content)
        self.assertIn("chmod 0640", content)
        self.assertNotIn("PGPASSWORD", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Adds a practical hardening control for backup operations and least-privilege file handling.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm generated backup artifacts use restrictive permissions.

tasks.title: Add backup restore checksum policy

files.content: Restore operations should verify a recorded checksum before applying a backup artifact. The helper is intentionally small, avoids credentials, and gives operators a clear failure when an artifact changes unexpectedly.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${BACKUP_ARTIFACT:?BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required}"
: "${BACKUP_CHECKSUM_FILE:?BACKUP_CHECKSUM_FILE is required}"
sha256sum --check "$BACKUP_CHECKSUM_FILE" --status
printf 'backup_checksum_verified=%s\n' "$(basename "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT")"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class BackupRestoreChecksumTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_requires_inputs_and_verifies_sha256(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/backup/templates/verify-backup-checksum.sh.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required", content)
        self.assertIn("BACKUP_CHECKSUM_FILE is required", content)
        self.assertIn("sha256sum --check", content)
        self.assertNotIn("PGPASSWORD", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Strengthens restore safety with an explicit integrity check before backup data is used.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm the checksum helper requires both artifact and checksum file.

tasks.title: Add backup freshness check template

files.content: A successful backup is not enough when the last artifact is stale. This template exposes a lightweight freshness check that monitoring can run without reading backup credentials or parsing application logs.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

: "${BACKUP_ARTIFACT:?BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required}"
MAX_AGE_MINUTES="{{ backup_max_age_minutes | default(1440) }}"
age_minutes=$(( ( $(date +%s) - $(stat -c %Y "$BACKUP_ARTIFACT") ) / 60 ))
if [ "$age_minutes" -gt "$MAX_AGE_MINUTES" ]; then
  echo "backup is stale: ${age_minutes}m" >&2
  exit 1
fi
printf 'backup_age_minutes=%s\n' "$age_minutes"

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class BackupFreshnessTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_checks_artifact_age_without_credentials(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/backup/templates/check-backup-freshness.sh.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("BACKUP_ARTIFACT is required", content)
        self.assertIn("backup_max_age_minutes", content)
        self.assertIn("backup is stale", content)
        self.assertNotIn("PGPASSWORD", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Adds a monitorable safeguard for stale backups and makes operational readiness visible in the repository.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm the freshness script requires an artifact and avoids credentials.

tasks.title: Add systemd resource limit template

files.content: Service-level resource limits prevent one workload from exhausting a host during an incident. The template keeps memory and file descriptor limits configurable and visible in version control.

import unittest
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


class SystemdResourceLimitsTemplateTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_template_declares_memory_file_and_task_limits(self):
        content = (ROOT / "roles/common/templates/systemd-resource-limits.conf.j2").read_text(encoding="utf-8")

        self.assertIn("MemoryMax", content)
        self.assertIn("LimitNOFILE", content)
        self.assertIn("TasksMax", content)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows Linux operational hardening includes service isolation, not only host-level configuration.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm all three resource controls are present.

tasks.title: Add restore drill evidence template

files.content: A restore drill should produce machine-readable evidence that identifies the backup artifact, records checksum verification, captures the test window, and states whether application-level validation passed. The evidence contains operational metadata only and no credentials.

tasks.portfolio_reason: Turns restore testing into auditable recovery evidence without exposing production secrets.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm the evidence template records integrity, timing, and application verification fields.
